text: As I step into this space, I am struck by a sense of curiosity and dislocation. The glass walls and vibrant colors create an atmosphere charged with possibility, but also with distance—the world beyond the glass feels both inviting and inaccessible. I am aware of my presence, of my act of observation, and I wonder about the boundaries between observer and participant. The displays speak of research and development, of innovation, yet there is an undercurrent of vulnerability and exposure, especially as I read the texts that voice confusion and longing for understanding. I feel both intrigued by the ideas around me and unsettled by the act of being seen, as if every movement and thought might be interpreted, recorded, or misunderstood.
